settings.text: A place built around one simple idea: every child deserves to feel safe, seen, and excited to learn.
Started by Parents, Run by Educators

Little Oaks was founded in 2011 by two parents who could not find a daycare that matched what they wanted for their own children: small groups, qualified teachers, real outdoor time, and a curriculum built around how children actually develop. What started as a single classroom with eight children has grown into a learning center serving over 120 families in Asheville.

settings.title: Nurturing Care
settings.description: Every child is known by name, temperament, and what makes them light up. Our teachers build relationships before they build lesson plans.

settings.title: Play-Based Learning
settings.description: Children learn best through exploration and discovery. Our curriculum is structured around play, not worksheets.

settings.title: Safety First
settings.description: Licensed, inspected, and staffed above state requirements. Background checks, CPR certification, and secure entry on every door.

settings.title: Community
settings.description: Families are part of Little Oaks, not just clients. We host parent events, share daily updates, and build lasting relationships.

settings.title: Meet the Team
settings.description: Every teacher at Little Oaks holds a degree in early childhood education and brings years of hands-on classroom experience.

settings.name: Claire Whitfield
settings.bio: M.Ed. in Early Childhood Development. 18 years in childcare. Co-founded Little Oaks because she believed Asheville needed a better option for families.

settings.name: Rosa Gutierrez
settings.bio: B.A. in Early Education. 10 years of preschool teaching. Brings music, storytelling, and bilingual learning into every day.

settings.name: David Kowalski
settings.bio: B.S. in Child Development. 7 years with toddlers. Believes in messy play, outdoor time, and letting children move at their own speed.

settings.name: Aisha Thompson
settings.bio: Certified Infant Care Specialist. 9 years caring for the youngest children. Calm, patient, and deeply attentive to each baby's cues.

settings.quote: The teachers know my son better than I expected. They notice when he is having a hard day and they know exactly how to help him through it. That kind of care is rare.
settings.name: Laura Chen
settings.title: Parent of a 2-year-old

settings.quote: We toured five daycares before Little Oaks. The moment we walked in, we knew. The space felt warm, the kids were engaged, and the teachers were genuinely present. Our daughter started the following week.
settings.name: Marcus and Tanya Hayes
settings.title: Parents of a 4-year-old

settings.text: See the classrooms, meet the teachers, and ask us anything.
